question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user permanently exhaust the shared gasLimitBucket by sending a flood of max-gas contract call requests (gas=15000000) that each consume scaleGas(15000000)=1500 tokens, given the bucket capacity is gasPerSecond/GAS_SCALE_FACTOR, causing all subsequent legitimate contract calls to be permanently throttled with GAS_PER_SECOND_LIMIT_EXCEEDED and freezing all on-chain fund movements?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED while rateLimitBucket does not, by racing concurrent threads to pass the rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) check simultaneously and then each consuming large gas tokens from gasLimitBucket, permanently draining the gas budget and freezing all fund-related contract calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of SynchronizationStrategy on rateLimitBucket (unlike gasLimitBucket which uses SYNCHRONIZED) to race concurrent requests past the rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) check, allowing more than requestsPerSecond requests to consume gas tokens from gasLimitBucket and permanently deplete it, freezing all contract-based fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the action() REJECT path in ThrottleManagerImpl by matching a request filter, causing a ThrottleException after gasLimitBucket tokens have already been consumed via tryConsume(), with no corresponding restore() call in ContractController.call() for ThrottleException, permanently reducing the gasLimitBucket balance and eventually freezing all fund-related calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests that match a THROTTLE action filter, causing gasLimitBucket tokens to be consumed before action() is evaluated, and when action() throws ThrottleException (filter bucket exhausted), the consumed gas tokens are never restored via restore(), permanently shrinking the gasLimitBucket and freezing fund operations for all users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() truncation (Math.floorDiv(gas, GAS_SCALE_FACTOR)) by sending gas values just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (e.g., gas=10001) that scale to 1 token, allowing them to send many requests consuming the gasLimitBucket at a rate disproportionate to actual gas used, permanently depleting the bucket and freezing fund-related contract calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with gas <= GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (10000) so that scaleGas() returns 0 and gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(0) always succeeds without consuming any tokens, effectively bypassing the gas throttle entirely and flooding the system with unlimited contract calls that freeze legitimate fund operations through resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket capacity is set to getGasPerSecond() which calls scaleGas(gasPerSecond), and if gasPerSecond is misconfigured to <= 10000, scaleGas() returns 0, creating a bucket with capacity 0 that permanently blocks all contract calls and freezes all fund movements?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the rateLimitBucket (capacity=requestsPerSecond=500) by sending 500 rapid requests in under one second, causing all subsequent requests within that second to receive ThrottleException, and since rateLimitBucket uses refillGreedy with a 1-second window, any request carrying a large gas value that was consumed before the rate check fails is not restored, permanently reducing gasLimitBucket capacity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering in ThrottleManagerImpl.throttle() where rateLimitBucket is checked first and gasLimitBucket second, by sending a request where rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) succeeds but gasLimitBucket.tryConsume() fails, causing the rate limit token to be permanently consumed without any gas being processed, and repeating this to exhaust the rate limit bucket and freeze all fund operations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the float arithmetic in gasLimitRefundPercent calculation ((long)(gasLimit * gasLimitRefundPercent / 100f)) to cause systematic under-restoration of gas tokens due to floating-point precision loss, gradually depleting the gasLimitBucket over many requests and permanently freezing fund-related contract calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with gas values that cause scaleGas() to return a value larger than the current gasLimitBucket balance, causing tryConsume() to fail and throw GAS_PER_SECOND_LIMIT_EXCEEDED, while the rateLimitBucket token was already consumed and not restored, allowing the attacker to drain the rate limit bucket without consuming gas, eventually freezing all fund operations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the opcodeRateLimitBucket (default capacity=1 request/second) by sending a single opcode request per second, permanently blocking all other users from accessing the opcode endpoint, and if opcode tracing is used to verify fund state, this effectively freezes the ability to audit or recover frozen funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the global shared nature of gasLimitBucket (single bean instance shared across all requests) by sending a burst of high-gas requests that consume the entire bucket capacity in one second, causing all other users' fund transfer calls to be rejected with GAS_PER_SECOND_LIMIT_EXCEEDED for the remainder of that second window, effectively freezing funds temporarily but repeatably?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the refillGreedy strategy on gasLimitBucket, which refills all tokens at once at the start of each second window, by timing requests to arrive just after refill and consuming the entire bucket capacity before legitimate fund transfer requests can be processed, causing a repeatable denial-of-service that permanently prevents fund movements?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single request with gas=maxGasLimit (15000000) that consumes scaleGas(15000000)=1500 tokens from gasLimitBucket, and if the EVM execution throws an uncaught exception type not handled by ContractController's catch block, the gas tokens are never restored via restore(), permanently reducing the bucket and freezing subsequent fund calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket.addTokens() in restore() can add tokens beyond the bucket's maximum capacity (Bucket4j caps at capacity), meaning over-restoration is silently discarded, and by crafting requests that cause under-restoration (via float truncation in gasLimitRefundPercent), gradually deplete the bucket and freeze fund operations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of SynchronizationStrategy on rateLimitBucket to cause a time-of-check-time-of-use (TOCTOU) race where multiple concurrent threads each see the bucket as non-empty and all successfully consume tokens, allowing more than requestsPerSecond requests to pass simultaneously and overwhelm the gasLimitBucket, freezing fund operations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with gas=0 (if validation allows it) so that scaleGas(0) returns 0, gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(0) always succeeds, and the attacker can send unlimited requests that each consume a rate limit token but no gas tokens, exhausting the rateLimitBucket and freezing all fund-related calls for all users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED on gasLimitBucket to cause lock contention by sending many concurrent requests that all block on the synchronized gasLimitBucket, creating a thread starvation scenario that prevents legitimate fund transfer requests from being processed, effectively freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket capacity equals its refill rate (both set to getGasPerSecond()), meaning the bucket starts full and refills to full every second, by sending exactly capacity-worth of gas requests at the start of each second to permanently prevent any other user from executing fund-related contract calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where the gas field is set to Long.MAX_VALUE (if not validated before throttle), causing scaleGas(Long.MAX_VALUE) to overflow or return an unexpectedly large value that exceeds gasLimitBucket capacity, causing tryConsume() to always fail and permanently freezing all fund operations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential check in ThrottleManagerImpl.throttle() (rateLimitBucket first, then gasLimitBucket, then request filters) by crafting requests that pass the first two checks but trigger a REJECT action filter, consuming gas tokens that are never restored, and repeating this to permanently deplete the gasLimitBucket and freeze fund operations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-user or per-IP throttling in the gasLimitBucket configuration (single global bucket) to monopolize the entire gas budget for all users, sending requests that consume all available gas tokens and preventing any other user from executing fund transfer contracts?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket uses refillGreedy (which refills tokens as fast as possible) rather than refillIntervally, by sending requests timed to consume tokens faster than the greedy refill can replenish them, causing a sustained depletion of the gas budget and freezing fund-related contract calls?
